mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand, which lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors price in stronger economic momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.10 or negative for a two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a 5.5% threshold, it signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the proportion of positive 60-day SPX returns exceeds 50% or the average return is not statistically below unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and inflation (rising real rate) tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal (FEDFUNDS minus CPI growth) shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window or a significant proportion of contradictory instances where rising real rates coincide with positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, rising housing starts signal overheating in the construction sector, which precedes a pullback in consumer spending and a peak in equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During periods identified as late-cycle (based on output gap and credit spreads), HOUST increases are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The one-week forward return decile spread between high and low INDPRO growth months is less than 1.0% over a five-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI exceeds the 200 index level, it signals persistent inflation pressure that forces the Fed to tighten policy, hurting equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After a CPIAUCSL threshold breach, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% across all instances in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment signal a tight labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The composite labor signal (standardized PAYEMS growth minus UNRATE change) shows a negative or zero correlation with next-month SPX returns over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, the federal funds rate level proxies the restrictive stance of policy, which reduces liquidity and raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods identified as tightening cycles (consecutive FOMC rate hikes), the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day SPX returns is positive or nearly zero, or the FEDFUNDS-based signal fails to generate negative returns in more than 50% of instances.
